name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
description: Free, confidential 24/7 crisis support for people in suicidal crisis or mental health distress. Call or text 988.
eligibility: Anyone in crisis, free of charge

name: Crisis Text Line
description: Free crisis counseling via text message, 24/7. Text HOME to 741741.
eligibility: Anyone in crisis, free of charge

name: 211 Helpline
description: Free information and referral service connecting people with local health and human services. Covers housing, food, utilities, healthcare, and more.

name: National Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Confidential support for victims of domestic violence. Call, text, or chat online.
eligibility: Victims and survivors of domestic violence, free and confidential

name: SAMHSA National Helpline
description: Free, confidential, 24/7 treatment referral and information service for individuals and families facing mental and substance use disorders.
eligibility: Free and confidential, open to all

name: Feeding America Food Bank Network
description: Nationwide network of 200+ food banks serving 46 million people. Use the locator to find your nearest food bank or pantry.

name: HUD Housing Counseling
description: HUD-approved housing counselors help with renting, defaults, foreclosures, credit issues, and reverse mortgages.

name: NAMI Helpline
description: National Alliance on Mental Illness helpline providing information, referrals, and support for people affected by mental illness.
eligibility: People with mental illness and their families, free

name: Benefits.gov Federal Benefits Finder
description: Official U.S. government site to find federal and state benefits including SNAP, Medicaid, CHIP, WIC, housing, and disability programs.

name: LawHelp.org Legal Aid Finder
description: Directory of free legal aid organizations for low-income people covering housing, family law, immigration, benefits, and consumer issues.
eligibility: Low-income individuals, free or reduced-fee services

name: Veterans Crisis Line
description: Confidential crisis support for veterans, service members, and their families. Call 988 and press 1, or text 838255.
eligibility: Veterans, service members, and their families

name: Childhelp National Child Abuse Hotline
description: Crisis intervention, information, and referrals to emergency, social service, and support resources for child abuse situations.
eligibility: Anyone concerned about a child's safety

name: The Trevor Project
description: Crisis intervention and suicide prevention for LGBTQ+ young people. Call, text, or chat.
eligibility: LGBTQ+ youth under 25, free and confidential

name: LIHEAP Low Income Home Energy Assistance
description: Federally funded program that helps low-income households pay heating and cooling bills and make energy-related home repairs.
eligibility: Low-income households, income limits vary by state
